NKJV.1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, To the church of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints who are in all Achaia:
NKJV.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort,
NKJV.4: who comforts us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to comfort those who are in any trouble, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
NKJV.5: For as the sufferings of Christ abound in us, so our consolation also abounds through Christ.
NKJV.6: Now if we are afflicted, it is for your consolation and salvation, which is effective for enduring the same sufferings which we also suffer. Or if we are comforted, it is for your consolation and salvation.
NKJV.7: And our hope for you is steadfast, because we know that as you are partakers of the sufferings, so also you will partake of the consolation.
NKJV.8: For we do not want you to be ignorant, brethren, of our trouble which came to us in Asia: that we were burdened beyond measure, above strength, so that we despaired even of life.
NKJV.9: Yes, we had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves but in God who raises the dead,
NKJV.10: who delivered us from so great a death, and does deliver us; in whom we trust that He will still deliver us,
NKJV.11: you also helping together in prayer for us, that thanks may be given by many persons on our behalf for the gift granted to us through many.
NKJV.12: For our boasting is this: the testimony of our conscience that we conducted ourselves in the world in simplicity and godly sincerity, not with fleshly wisdom but by the grace of God, and more abundantly toward you.
NKJV.13: For we are not writing any other things to you than what you read or understand. Now I trust you will understand, even to the end
NKJV.14: (as also you have understood us in part), that we are your boast as you also are ours, in the day of the Lord Jesus.
NKJV.15: And in this confidence I intended to come to you before, that you might have a second benefit—
NKJV.16: to pass by way of you to Macedonia, to come again from Macedonia to you, and be helped by you on my way to Judea.
NKJV.17: Therefore, when I was planning this, did I do it lightly? Or the things I plan, do I plan according to the flesh, that with me there should be Yes, Yes, and No, No?
NKJV.18: But as God is faithful, our word to you was not Yes and No.
NKJV.19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us—by me, Silvanus, and Timothy—was not Yes and No, but in Him was Yes.
NKJV.20: For all the promises of God in Him are Yes, and in Him Amen, to the glory of God through us.
NKJV.21: Now He who establishes us with you in Christ and has anointed us is God,
NKJV.22: who also has sealed us and given us the Spirit in our hearts as a guarantee.
NKJV.23: Moreover I call God as witness against my soul, that to spare you I came no more to Corinth.
NKJV.24: Not that we have dominion over your faith, but are fellow workers for your joy; for by faith you stand.

ESV.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, To the church of God that is at Corinth, with all the saints who are in the whole of Achaia:
ESV.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort,
ESV.4: who comforts us in all our affliction, so that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
ESV.5: For as we share abundantly in Christ's sufferings, so through Christ we share abundantly in comfort too.
ESV.6: If we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation; and if we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which you experience when you patiently endure the same sufferings that we suffer.
ESV.7: Our hope for you is unshaken, for we know that as you share in our sufferings, you will also share in our comfort.
ESV.8: For we do not want you to be unaware, brothers,  of the affliction we experienced in Asia. For we were so utterly burdened beyond our strength that we despaired of life itself.
ESV.9: Indeed, we felt that we had received the sentence of death. But that was to make us rely not on ourselves but on God who raises the dead.
ESV.10: He delivered us from such a deadly peril, and he will deliver us. On him we have set our hope that he will deliver us again.
ESV.11: You also must help us by prayer, so that many will give thanks on our behalf for the blessing granted us through the prayers of many.
ESV.12: For our boast is this, the testimony of our conscience, that we behaved in the world with simplicity  and godly sincerity, not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God, and supremely so toward you.
ESV.13: For we are not writing to you anything other than what you read and understand and I hope you will fully understand —
ESV.14: just as you did partially understand us — that on the day of our Lord Jesus you will boast of us as we will boast of you.
ESV.15: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to come to you first, so that you might have a second experience of grace.
ESV.16: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and to come back to you from Macedonia and have you send me on my way to Judea.
ESV.17: Was I vacillating when I wanted to do this? Do I make my plans according to the flesh, ready to say “Yes, yes” and “No, no” at the same time?
ESV.18: As surely as God is faithful, our word to you has not been Yes and No.
ESV.19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, whom we proclaimed among you, Silvanus and Timothy and I, was not Yes and No, but in him it is always Yes.
ESV.20: For all the promises of God find their Yes in him. That is why it is through him that we utter our Amen to God for his glory.
ESV.21: And it is God who establishes us with you in Christ, and has anointed us,
ESV.22: and who has also put his seal on us and given us his Spirit in our hearts as a guarantee.
ESV.23: But I call God to witness against me — it was to spare you that I refrained from coming again to Corinth.
ESV.24: Not that we lord it over your faith, but we work with you for your joy, for you stand firm in your faith.

NASB.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy [our] brother, To the church of God which is at Corinth with all the saints who are throughout Achaia:
NASB.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.3: Blessed [be] the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort,
NASB.4: who comforts us in all our affliction so that we will be able to comfort those who are in any affliction with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
NASB.5: For just as the sufferings of Christ are ours in abundance, so also our comfort is abundant through Christ.
NASB.6: But if we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation; or if we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which is effective in the patient enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer;
NASB.7: and our hope for you is firmly grounded, knowing that as you are sharers of our sufferings, so also you are [sharers] of our comfort.
NASB.8: For we do not want you to be unaware, brethren, of our affliction which came [to us] in Asia, that we were burdened excessively, beyond our strength, so that we despaired even of life;
NASB.9: indeed, we had the sentence of death within ourselves so that we would not trust in ourselves, but in God who raises the dead;
NASB.10: who delivered us from so great a [peril of] death, and will deliver [us], He on whom we have set our hope. And He will yet deliver us,
NASB.11: you also joining in helping us through your prayers, so that thanks may be given by many persons on our behalf for the favor bestowed on us through [the prayers] [of] many.
NASB.12: For our proud confidence is this: the testimony of our conscience, that in holiness and godly sincerity, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God, we have conducted ourselves in the world, and especially toward you.
NASB.13: For we write nothing else to you than what you read and understand, and I hope you will understand until the end;
NASB.14: just as you also partially did understand us, that we are your reason to be proud as you also are ours, in the day of our Lord Jesus.
NASB.15: In this confidence I intended at first to come to you, so that you might twice receive a blessing;
NASB.16: that is, to pass your way into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and by you to be helped on my journey to Judea.
NASB.17: Therefore, I was not vacillating when I intended to do this, was I? Or what I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, so that with me there will be yes, yes and no, no [at the same time]?
NASB.18: But as God is faithful, our word to you is not yes and no.
NASB.19: For the Son of God, Christ Jesus, who was preached among you by us-- by me and Silvanus and Timothy-- was not yes and no, but is yes in Him.
NASB.20: For as many as are the promises of God, in Him they are yes; therefore also through Him is our Amen to the glory of God through us.
NASB.21: Now He who establishes us with you in Christ and anointed us is God,
NASB.22: who also sealed us and gave [us] the Spirit in our hearts as a pledge.
NASB.23: But I call God as witness to my soul, that to spare you I did not come again to Corinth.
NASB.24: Not that we lord it over your faith, but are workers with you for your joy; for in your faith you are standing firm.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, chosen by the will of God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from our brother Timothy. I am writing to God’s church in Corinth and to all of his holy people throughout Greece.
NLT.2: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
NLT.3: All praise to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God is our merciful Father and the source of all comfort.
NLT.4: He comforts us in all our troubles so that we can comfort others. When they are troubled, we will be able to give them the same comfort God has given us.
NLT.5: For the more we suffer for Christ, the more God will shower us with his comfort through Christ.
NLT.6: Even when we are weighed down with troubles, it is for your comfort and salvation! For when we ourselves are comforted, we will certainly comfort you. Then you can patiently endure the same things we suffer.
NLT.7: We are confident that as you share in our sufferings, you will also share in the comfort God gives us.
NLT.8: We think you ought to know, dear brothers and sisters, about the trouble we went through in the province of Asia. We were crushed and overwhelmed beyond our ability to endure, and we thought we would never live through it.
NLT.9: In fact, we expected to die. But as a result, we stopped relying on ourselves and learned to rely only on God, who raises the dead.
NLT.10: And he did rescue us from mortal danger, and he will rescue us again. We have placed our confidence in him, and he will continue to rescue us.
NLT.11: And you are helping us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks because God has graciously answered so many prayers for our safety.
NLT.12: We can say with confidence and a clear conscience that we have lived with a God-given holiness and sincerity in all our dealings. We have depended on God’s grace, not on our own human wisdom. That is how we have conducted ourselves before the world, and especially toward you.
NLT.13: Our letters have been straightforward, and there is nothing written between the lines and nothing you can’t understand. I hope someday you will fully understand us,
NLT.14: even if you don’t understand us now. Then on the day when the Lord Jesus returns, you will be proud of us in the same way we are proud of you.
NLT.15: Since I was so sure of your understanding and trust, I wanted to give you a double blessing by visiting you twice —
NLT.16: first on my way to Macedonia and again when I returned from Macedonia. Then you could send me on my way to Judea.
NLT.17: You may be asking why I changed my plan. Do you think I make my plans carelessly? Do you think I am like people of the world who say “Yes” when they really mean “No”?
NLT.18: As surely as God is faithful, our word to you does not waver between “Yes” and “No.”
NLT.19: For Jesus Christ, the Son of God, does not waver between “Yes” and “No.” He is the one whom Silas, Timothy, and I preached to you, and as God’s ultimate “Yes,” he always does what he says.
NLT.20: For all of God’s promises have been fulfilled in Christ with a resounding “Yes!” And through Christ, our “Amen” (which means “Yes”) ascends to God for his glory.
NLT.21: It is God who enables us, along with you, to stand firm for Christ. He has commissioned us,
NLT.22: and he has identified us as his own by placing the Holy Spirit in our hearts as the first installment that guarantees everything he has promised us.
NLT.23: Now I call upon God as my witness that I am telling the truth. The reason I didn’t return to Corinth was to spare you from a severe rebuke.
NLT.24: But that does not mean we want to dominate you by telling you how to put your faith into practice. We want to work together with you so you will be full of joy, for it is by your own faith that you stand firm.

WEB.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother, to the assembly of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints who are in the whole of Achaia:
WEB.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort;
WEB.4: who comforts us in all our affliction, that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, through the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
WEB.5: For as the sufferings of Christ abound to us, even so our comfort also abounds through Christ.
WEB.6: But if we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which produces in you the patient enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer.
WEB.7: Our hope for you is steadfast, knowing that, since you are partakers of the sufferings, so also are you of the comfort.
WEB.8: For we don’t desire to have you uninformed, brothers,  concerning our affliction which happened to us in Asia, that we were weighed down exceedingly, beyond our power, so much that we despaired even of life.
WEB.9: Yes, we ourselves have had the sentence of death within ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God who raises the dead,
WEB.10: who delivered us out of so great a death, and does deliver; on whom we have set our hope that he will also still deliver us;
WEB.11: you also helping together on our behalf by your supplication; that, for the gift given to us by means of many, thanks may be given by many persons on your behalf.
WEB.12: For our boasting is this: the testimony of our conscience, that in holiness and sincerity of God, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God we behaved ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.
WEB.13: For we write no other things to you, than what you read or even acknowledge, and I hope you will acknowledge to the end;
WEB.14: as also you acknowledged us in part, that we are your boasting, even as you also are ours, in the day of our Lord Jesus.
WEB.15: In this confidence, I was determined to come first to you, that you might have a second benefit;
WEB.16: and by you to pass into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and to be sent forward by you on my journey to Judea.
WEB.17: When I therefore was thus determined, did I show fickleness? Or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be the “Yes, yes” and the “No, no?”
WEB.18: But as God is faithful, our word toward you was not “Yes and no.”
WEB.19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, by me, Silvanus, and Timothy, was not “Yes and no,” but in him is “Yes.”
WEB.20: For however many are the promises of God, in him is the “Yes.” Therefore also through him is the “Amen”, to the glory of God through us.
WEB.21: Now he who establishes us with you in Christ, and anointed us, is God;
WEB.22: who also sealed us, and gave us the down payment of the Spirit in our hearts.
WEB.23: But I call God for a witness to my soul, that I didn’t come to Corinth to spare you.
WEB.24: Not that we control your faith, but are fellow workers with you for your joy. For you stand firm in faith.

CSB17.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by God’s will, <sup>ⓒ</sup> and Timothy <sup>ⓓ</sup> our <sup>[1]</sup> brother: To the church of God at Corinth, <sup>ⓔ</sup> with all the saints who are throughout Achaia. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: Grace to you and peace <sup>ⓖ</sup> from God our Father <sup>ⓗ</sup> and the Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.3: Blessed be <sup>ⓘ</sup> the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies <sup>ⓙ</sup> and the God of all comfort. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.4: He comforts us in all our affliction, <sup>[2]</sup> so that we may be able to comfort those who are in any kind of affliction, through the comfort we ourselves receive from God.
CSB17.5: For just as the sufferings <sup>ⓛ</sup> of Christ <sup>ⓜ</sup> overflow to us, so also through Christ our comfort overflows. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.6: If we are afflicted, <sup>ⓐ</sup> it is for your comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which produces in you patient endurance of the same sufferings that we suffer. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.7: And our hope <sup>ⓒ</sup> for you is firm, because we know that as you share <sup>ⓓ</sup> in the sufferings, so you will also share in the comfort.
CSB17.8: We don’t want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, of our affliction that took place in Asia. <sup>ⓔ</sup> We were completely overwhelmed — beyond our strength <sup>ⓕ</sup>  — so that we even despaired <sup>ⓖ</sup> of life itself.
CSB17.9: Indeed, we felt that we had received the sentence of death, so that we would not trust in ourselves but in God <sup>ⓗ</sup> who raises <sup>ⓘ</sup> the dead.
CSB17.10: He has delivered <sup>ⓙ</sup> us from such a terrible death, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and he will deliver us. We have put our hope in him that he will deliver us again
CSB17.11: while you join in helping us by your prayers. Then many will give thanks on our <sup>[3]</sup> behalf for the gift that came to us through the prayers of many.
CSB17.12: Indeed, this is our boast: The testimony <sup>ⓛ</sup> of our conscience <sup>ⓜ</sup> is that we have conducted ourselves in the world, and especially toward you, with godly sincerity and purity, not by human wisdom <sup>ⓝ</sup> but by God’s grace. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.13: For we are writing nothing to you other than what you can read and also understand. I hope you will understand completely —
CSB17.14: just as you have partially understood us — that we are your reason for pride, <sup>ⓑ</sup> just as you also are ours <sup>ⓒ</sup> in the day of our <sup>[4]</sup> Lord Jesus. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.15: Because of this confidence, I planned to come to you first, <sup>ⓔ</sup> so that you could have a second benefit, <sup>[5]</sup>
CSB17.16: and to visit you on my way to Macedonia, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and then come to you again from Macedonia and be helped by you <sup>ⓖ</sup> on my journey <sup>ⓗ</sup> to Judea. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.17: Now when I planned this, was I of two minds? Or what I plan, do I plan in a purely human <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> way so that I say “Yes, yes” and “No, no” at the same time?
CSB17.18: As God is faithful, <sup>ⓚ</sup> our message to you is not “Yes and no.”
CSB17.19: For the Son of God, <sup>ⓛ</sup> Jesus Christ, whom we proclaimed among you — Silvanus, <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup> Timothy, and I — did not become “Yes and no.” On the contrary, in him it is always “Yes.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.20: For every one of God’s promises <sup>ⓐ</sup> is “Yes” in him. Therefore, through him we also say “Amen” <sup>ⓑ</sup> to the glory of God. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.21: Now it is God who strengthens us together with you in Christ, and who has anointed <sup>ⓓ</sup> us.
CSB17.22: He has also put his seal on us and given us the Spirit in our hearts <sup>ⓔ</sup> as a down payment. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.23: I call on God as a witness, <sup>ⓖ</sup> on my life, that it was to spare you that I did not come to Corinth. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.24: I do not mean that we lord it over your faith, but we are workers with you for your joy, because you stand firm in your <sup>ⓘ</sup> faith.

AMP.1:
Paul, an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Christ Jesus (the Messiah) by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
To the church of God which is at Corinth, and to all the saints (God's people) throughout Achaia (southern Greece):
AMP.2: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.3: Blessed [gratefully praised and adored] be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and the God of all comfort,
AMP.4: who comforts and encourages us in every trouble so that we will be able to comfort and encourage those who are in any kind of trouble, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
AMP.5: For just as Christ's sufferings are ours in abundance [as they overflow to His followers], so also our comfort [our reassurance, our encouragement, our consolation] is abundant through Christ [it is truly more than enough to endure what we must].
AMP.6: But if we are troubled and distressed, it is for your comfort and salvation; or if we are comforted and encouraged, it is for your comfort, which works [in you] when you patiently endure the same sufferings which we experience.
AMP.7: And our hope for you [our confident expectation of good for you] is firmly grounded [assured and unshaken], since we know that just as you share as partners in our sufferings, so also you share as partners in our comfort.
AMP.8: For we do not want you to be uninformed, brothers and sisters, about our trouble in [the west coast province of] Asia [Minor], how we were utterly weighed down, beyond our strength, so that we despaired even of life [itself].
AMP.9: Indeed, we felt within ourselves that we had received the sentence of death [and were convinced that we would die, but this happened] so that we would not trust in ourselves, but in God who raises the dead.
AMP.10: He rescued us from so great a threat of death, and will continue to rescue us. On Him we have set our hope. And He will again rescue us [from danger and draw us near],
AMP.11: while you join in helping us by your prayers. Then thanks will be given by many persons on our behalf for the gracious gift [of deliverance] granted to us through the prayers of many [believers].
AMP.12: This is our [reason for] proud confidence: our conscience testifies that we have conducted ourselves in the world [in general], and especially toward you, with pure motives and godly sincerity, not in human wisdom, but in the grace of God [that is, His gracious lovingkindness that leads people to Christ and spiritual maturity].
AMP.13: For we write you nothing other than what you read and understand [there is no double meaning in what we say]. And I hope you will [accurately] understand [divine things] until the end;
AMP.14: just as you have [already] partially understood us, [and one day will recognize] that you can be proud of us just as we are of you, in the day of our Lord Jesus.
AMP.15: It was with this confidence that I planned at first to visit you, so that you might receive twice a token of grace;
AMP.16: that is, [I wanted] to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and [then] to come back to you [on my return] from Macedonia, and have you send me on my way to Judea.
AMP.17: So then, was I indecisive or capricious when I was [originally] planning this? Or the things I plan, do I plan in a self-serving way like a worldly man, ready to say, "Yes, yes" and "No, no" [at the same time]?
AMP.18: But [as surely as] God is faithful and means what He says, our message to you is not "Yes" and "No" [at the same time].
AMP.19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, by me, Silvanus, and Timothy, was not "Yes" and "No," but has proved to be "Yes" in Him [true and faithful, the divine "Yes" affirming God's promises].
AMP.20: For as many as are the promises of God, in Christ they are [all answered] "Yes." So through Him we say our "Amen" to the glory of God.
AMP.21: Now it is God who establishes and confirms us [in joint fellowship] with you in Christ, and who has anointed us [empowering us with the gifts of the Spirit];
AMP.22: it is He who has also put His seal on us [that is, He has appropriated us and certified us as His] and has given us the [Holy] Spirit in our hearts as a pledge [like a security deposit to guarantee the fulfillment of His promise of eternal life].
AMP.23: But I call on God as my soul's witness, that it was to spare you [pain and discouragement] that I did not come again to Corinth—
AMP.24: not that we rule [like dictators] over your faith, but rather we work with you for [the increase of] your joy; for in your faith you stand firm [in your strong conviction that Jesus of Nazareth—the Messiah—is the Son of God, through whom we obtain eternal salvation].

MSG.1: I, Paul, have been sent on a special mission by the Messiah, Jesus, planned by God himself. I write this to God's congregation in Corinth, and to believers all over Achaia province.
MSG.2: May all the gifts and benefits that come from God our Father and the Master, Jesus Christ, be yours! Timothy, someone you know and trust, joins me in this greeting.
MSG.3: All praise to the God and Father of our Master, Jesus the Messiah! Father of all mercy! God of all healing counsel!
MSG.4: He comes alongside us when we go through hard times, and before you know it, he brings us alongside someone else who is going through hard times so that we can be there for that person just as God was there for us.
MSG.5: We have plenty of hard times that come from following the Messiah, but no more so than the good times of his healing comfort—we get a full measure of that, too.
MSG.6: When we suffer for Jesus, it works out for your healing and salvation. If we are treated well, given a helping hand and encouraging word, that also works to your benefit, spurring you on, face forward, unflinching. Your hard times are also our hard times.
MSG.7: When we see that you're just as willing to endure the hard times as to enjoy the good times, we know you're going to make it, no doubt about it.
MSG.8: We don't want you in the dark, friends, about how hard it was when all this came down on us in Asia province. It was so bad we didn't think we were going to make it.
MSG.9: We felt like we'd been sent to death row, that it was all over for us. As it turned out, it was the best thing that could have happened. Instead of trusting in our own strength or wits to get out of it, we were forced to trust God totally—not a bad idea since he's the God who raises the dead!
MSG.10: And he did it, rescued us from certain doom. And he'll do it again, rescuing us as many times as we need rescuing.
MSG.11: You and your prayers are part of the rescue operation—I don't want you in the dark about that either. I can see your faces even now, lifted in praise for God's deliverance of us, a rescue in which your prayers played such a crucial part.
MSG.12: Now that the worst is over, we're pleased we can report that we've come out of this with conscience and faith intact, and can face the world—and even more importantly, face you with our heads held high. But it wasn't by any fancy footwork on our part. It was God who kept us focused on him, uncompromised.
MSG.13: Don't try to read between the lines or look for hidden meanings in this letter. We're writing plain, unembellished truth, hoping that
MSG.14: you'll now see the whole picture as well as you've seen some of the details. We want you to be as proud of us as we are of you when we stand together before our Master Jesus.
MSG.15: Confident of your welcome, I had originally planned two great visits with you—
MSG.16: coming by on my way to Macedonia province, and then again on my return trip. Then we could have had a bon-voyage party as you sent me off to Judea. That was the plan.
MSG.17: Are you now going to accuse me of being flip with my promises because it didn't work out? Do you think I talk out of both sides of my mouth—a glib yes one moment, a glib no the next?
MSG.18: Well, you're wrong. I try to be as true to my word as God is to his. Our word to you wasn't a careless yes canceled by an indifferent no. How could it be?
MSG.19: When Silas and Timothy and I proclaimed the Son of God among you, did you pick up on any yes-and-no, on-again, off-again waffling? Wasn't it a clean, strong Yes?
MSG.20: Whatever God has promised gets stamped with the Yes of Jesus. In him, this is what we preach and pray, the great Amen, God's Yes and our Yes together, gloriously evident.
MSG.21: God affirms us, making us a sure thing in Christ, putting his Yes within us.
MSG.22: By his Spirit he has stamped us with his eternal pledge—a sure beginning of what he is destined to complete.
MSG.23: Now, are you ready for the real reason I didn't visit you in Corinth? As God is my witness, the only reason I didn't come was to spare you pain. I was being considerate of you, not indifferent, not manipulative.
MSG.24: We're not in charge of how you live out the faith, looking over your shoulders, suspiciously critical. We're partners, working alongside you, joyfully expectant. I know that you stand by your own faith, not by ours.

NIV.1:
Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
To the church of God in Corinth, together with all the saints throughout Achaia:
NIV.2: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.3:
The God of All Comfort
Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of compassion and the God of all comfort,
NIV.4: who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves have received from God.
NIV.5: For just as the sufferings of Christ flow over into our lives, so also through Christ our comfort overflows.
NIV.6: If we are distressed, it is for your comfort and salvation; if we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which produces in you patient endurance of the same sufferings we suffer.
NIV.7: And our hope for you is firm, because we know that just as you share in our sufferings, so also you share in our comfort.
NIV.8: We do not want you to be uninformed, brothers, about the hardships we suffered in the province of Asia. We were under great pressure, far beyond our ability to endure, so that we despaired even of life.
NIV.9: Indeed, in our hearts we felt the sentence of death. But this happened that we might not rely on ourselves but on God, who raises the dead.
NIV.10: He has delivered us from such a deadly peril, and he will deliver us. On him we have set our hope that he will continue to deliver us,
NIV.11: as you help us by your prayers. Then many will give thanks on our behalf for the gracious favor granted us in answer to the prayers of many.
NIV.12:
Paul’s Change of Plans
Now this is our boast: Our conscience testifies that we have conducted ourselves in the world, and especially in our relations with you, in the holiness and sincerity that are from God. We have done so not according to worldly wisdom but according to God’s grace.
NIV.13: For we do not write you anything you cannot read or understand. And I hope that,
NIV.14: as you have understood us in part, you will come to understand fully that you can boast of us just as we will boast of you in the day of the Lord Jesus.
NIV.15: Because I was confident of this, I planned to visit you first so that you might benefit twice.
NIV.16: I planned to visit you on my way to Macedonia and to come back to you from Macedonia, and then to have you send me on my way to Judea.
NIV.17: When I planned this, did I do it lightly? Or do I make my plans in a worldly manner so that in the same breath I say, “Yes, yes” and “No, no”?
NIV.18: But as surely as God is faithful, our message to you is not “Yes” and “No.”
NIV.19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by me and Silas and Timothy, was not “Yes” and “No,” but in him it has always been “Yes.”
NIV.20: For no matter how many promises God has made, they are “Yes” in Christ. And so through him the “Amen” is spoken by us to the glory of God.
NIV.21: Now it is God who makes both us and you stand firm in Christ. He anointed us,
NIV.22: set his seal of ownership on us, and put his Spirit in our hearts as a deposit, guaranteeing what is to come.
NIV.23: I call God as my witness that it was in order to spare you that I did not return to Corinth.
NIV.24: Not that we lord it over your faith, but we work with you for your joy, because it is by faith you stand firm.

NRSVCE.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, To the church of God that is in Corinth, including all the saints throughout Achaia:
NRSVCE.2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and the God of all consolation,
NRSVCE.4: who consoles us in all our affliction, so that we may be able to console those who are in any affliction with the consolation with which we ourselves are consoled by God.
NRSVCE.5: For just as the sufferings of Christ are abundant for us, so also our consolation is abundant through Christ.
NRSVCE.6: If we are being afflicted, it is for your consolation and salvation; if we are being consoled, it is for your consolation, which you experience when you patiently endure the same sufferings that we are also suffering.
NRSVCE.7: Our hope for you is unshaken; for we know that as you share in our sufferings, so also you share in our consolation.
NRSVCE.8: We do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, of the affliction we experienced in Asia; for we were so utterly, unbearably crushed that we despaired of life itself.
NRSVCE.9: Indeed, we felt that we had received the sentence of death so that we would rely not on ourselves but on God who raises the dead.
NRSVCE.10: He who rescued us from so deadly a peril will continue to rescue us; on him we have set our hope that he will rescue us again,
NRSVCE.11: as you also join in helping us by your prayers, so that many will give thanks on our behalf for the blessing granted us through the prayers of many.
NRSVCE.12: Indeed, this is our boast, the testimony of our conscience: we have behaved in the world with frankness and godly sincerity, not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God — and all the more toward you.
NRSVCE.13: For we write you nothing other than what you can read and also understand; I hope you will understand until the end —
NRSVCE.14: as you have already understood us in part — that on the day of the Lord Jesus we are your boast even as you are our boast.
NRSVCE.15: Since I was sure of this, I wanted to come to you first, so that you might have a double favor;
NRSVCE.16: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and to come back to you from Macedonia and have you send me on to Judea.
NRSVCE.17: Was I vacillating when I wanted to do this? Do I make my plans according to ordinary human standards, ready to say “Yes, yes” and “No, no” at the same time?
NRSVCE.18: As surely as God is faithful, our word to you has not been “Yes and No.”
NRSVCE.19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, whom we proclaimed among you, Silvanus and Timothy and I, was not “Yes and No”; but in him it is always “Yes.”
NRSVCE.20: For in him every one of God’s promises is a “Yes.” For this reason it is through him that we say the “Amen,” to the glory of God.
NRSVCE.21: But it is God who establishes us with you in Christ and has anointed us,
NRSVCE.22: by putting his seal on us and giving us his Spirit in our hearts as a first installment.
NRSVCE.23: But I call on God as witness against me: it was to spare you that I did not come again to Corinth.
NRSVCE.24: I do not mean to imply that we lord it over your faith; rather, we are workers with you for your joy, because you stand firm in the faith.

NET.1: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, to the church of God that is in Corinth, with all the saints who are in all Achaia.
NET.2: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ!
NET.3: Blessed is the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort,
NET.4: who comforts us in all our troubles so that we may be able to comfort those experiencing any trouble with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
NET.5: For just as the sufferings of Christ overflow toward us, so also our comfort through Christ overflows to you.
NET.6: But if we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation; if we are comforted, it is for your comfort that you experience in your patient endurance of the same sufferings that we also suffer.
NET.7: And our hope for you is steadfast because we know that as you share in our sufferings, so also you will share in our comfort.
NET.8: For we do not want you to be unaware, brothers and sisters, regarding the affliction that happened to us in the province of Asia, that we were burdened excessively, beyond our strength, so that we despaired even of living.
NET.9: Indeed we felt as if the sentence of death had been passed against us, so that we would not trust in ourselves but in God who raises the dead.
NET.10: He delivered us from so great a risk of death, and he will deliver us. We have set our hope on him that he will deliver us yet again,
NET.11: as you also join in helping us by prayer, so that many people may give thanks to God on our behalf for the gracious gift given to us through the help of many.
NET.12: For our reason for confidence is this: the testimony of our conscience, that with pure motives and sincerity which are from God — not by human wisdom but by the grace of God — we conducted ourselves in the world, and all the more toward you.
NET.13: For we do not write you anything other than what you can read and also understand. But I hope that you will understand completely
NET.14: just as also you have partly understood us, that we are your source of pride just as you also are ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.
NET.15: And with this confidence I intended to come to you first so that you would get a second opportunity to see us,
NET.16: and through your help to go on into Macedonia and then from Macedonia to come back to you and be helped on our way into Judea by you.
NET.17: Therefore when I was planning to do this, I did not do so without thinking about what I was doing, did I? Or do I make my plans according to mere human standards so that I would be saying both “Yes, yes” and “No, no” at the same time?
NET.18: But as God is faithful, our message to you is not “Yes” and “No.”
NET.19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, the one who was proclaimed among you by us — by me and Silvanus and Timothy — was not “Yes” and “No,” but it has always been “Yes” in him.
NET.20: For every one of God’s promises are “Yes” in him; therefore also through him the “Amen” is spoken, to the glory we give to God.
NET.21: But it is God who establishes us together with you in Christ and who anointed us,
NET.22: who also sealed us and gave us the Spirit in our hearts as a down payment.
NET.23: Now I appeal to God as my witness, that to spare you I did not come again to Corinth.
NET.24: I do not mean that we rule over your faith, but we are workers with you for your joy, because by faith you stand firm.
